inline_review:
commit b4b182a517dbdbf6ad02d5370d8d4ab0fc85e1b3
Author: Sourav Panda <souravpanda@google.com>

mm/hugetlb: skip hugetlb shrinking for proactive reclaim

Scan control can indicate if we are in the proactive reclaim mode. Pass that
to shrinker control and preclude frozen memory hugetlb shrinking if set.

> diff --git a/include/linux/shrinker.h b/include/linux/shrinker.h
> --- a/include/linux/shrinker.h
> +++ b/include/linux/shrinker.h
> @@ -52,6 +52,7 @@ struct shrink_control {
>  	unsigned long nr_scanned;
>  
>  	s8 priority;
> +	bool proactive;
>  
>  	/* current memcg being shrunk (for memcg aware shrinkers) */
>  	struct mem_cgroup *memcg;

Does adding this field leave it uninitialized in other parts of the kernel?

For example, in drivers/md/bcache/sysfs.c, a struct shrink_control sc is
allocated on the stack and only gfp_mask and nr_to_scan are explicitly
assigned. Adding the proactive field expands the uninitialized data footprint.

While the bcache shrinker callbacks do not currently read sc->proactive,
passing a partially uninitialized stack structure into a function pointer
could be brittle. Would it be safer to zero-initialize the struct?

> diff --git a/mm/hugetlb.c b/mm/hugetlb.c
> --- a/mm/hugetlb.c
> +++ b/mm/hugetlb.c
> @@ -4119,6 +4119,9 @@ static unsigned long hugepage_shrinker_count(struct shrinker *s,
>  	if (sc->priority >= DEF_PRIORITY - 6)
>  		return 0;
>  
> +	if (sc->proactive)
> +		return 0;
> +

While reviewing the hugepage_shrinker modifications, I noticed a pre-existing
behavior. The hugepage_shrinker_count() and hugepage_shrinker_scan()
functions explicitly use sc->nid to operate on a specific NUMA node's
hugepages.

However, the shrinker is registered without the SHRINKER_NUMA_AWARE flag.
Does this cause the shrinker framework to force all deferred work from all
NUMA nodes into a single bucket, erroneously aggregating and applying it to
node 0 during subsequent reclaim cycles?

Should SHRINKER_NUMA_AWARE be added to fix this?

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/mm/shrinker.c b/mm/shrinker.c
> --- a/mm/shrinker.c
> +++ b/mm/shrinker.c
> @@ -616,7 +617,7 @@ static unsigned long shrink_slab_memcg(gfp_t gfp_mask, int nid,
>   * Returns the number of reclaimed slab objects.
>   */
>  unsigned long shrink_slab(gfp_t gfp_mask, int nid, struct mem_cgroup *memcg,
> -			  int priority)
> +			  int priority, bool proactive)
>  {

Is it necessary to update the kernel-doc comment block above shrink_slab()
to document the new proactive parameter?

Leaving it out might trigger a kernel-doc warning during documentation
generation.
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Missing API Documentation for `proactive` parameter in `shrink_slab()`","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"The patch modifies the signature of `shrink_slab()` in `mm/shrinker.c` to include the new `bool proactive` parameter, but fails to update the corresponding kernel-doc comment block above it. This will trigger a kernel-doc warning during documentation generation and leaves the API incompletely specified."},{"problem":"Uninitialized `proactive` field usage in `drivers/md/bcache/sysfs.c`","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"In `drivers/md/bcache/sysfs.c` (e.g., `sysfs_prune_cache`), a `struct shrink_control sc;` is allocated on the stack and only `gfp_mask` and `nr_to_scan` are explicitly assigned. Adding the `proactive` field to `struct shrink_control` expands the uninitialized data footprint. While the bcache shrinker callbacks (`bch_mca_scan` and `bch_btree_cache_scan`) do not currently read `sc->proactive` (or `memcg`, `nid`, etc.), passing a partially uninitialized stack structure into a function pointer is a brittle anti-pattern. This should be fixed by zero-initializing the struct."},{"problem":"NUMA-awareness flag missing for `hugepage_shrinker` causes deferred work pollution","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"While reviewing the `hugepage_shrinker` modifications, it is evident that `hugepage_shrinker_count` and `hugepage_shrinker_scan` explicitly use `sc->nid` to operate on a specific NUMA node's hugepages (e.g., `h->nr_huge_pages_node[sc->nid]`). However, the shrinker is registered in `hugetlb_shrinker_init` via `shrinker_alloc(0, "hugetlbfs")` without the `SHRINKER_NUMA_AWARE` flag. Because of this omission, the shrinker framework forces all deferred work from all NUMA nodes into a single bucket (`shrinker->nr_deferred[0]`). Consequently, deferred work generated from failing to reclaim hugepages on one node is erroneously aggregated and applied to node 0 during subsequent reclaim cycles. Although this is a pre-existing issue in the hugetlb shrinker that the patch touches, it causes functional incorrectness in reclaim behavior and should be fixed by adding `SHRINKER_NUMA_AWARE`."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit b4b182a517dbdbf6ad02d5370d8d4ab0fc85e1b3
